frameworks.name: dynamical systems theory
frameworks.one_line: Mechanistic framework for time-varying neural activity and stability under perturbation

frameworks.name: stationary localized statistical analysis
frameworks.one_line: Standard neuroimaging approach that the paper argues is too static and local

frameworks.name: forward modeling of neural dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Maps neural dynamics by generating predicted activity from candidate mechanisms

core_question: How can dynamical systems theory reshape interpretation of human neuroimaging to better link it to neural mechanisms and behavior?
key_assumptions: Brain activity is time-varying yet partially stable under perturbation
Mechanistic interpretation requires moving beyond stationary statistical descriptions
Global, dynamic, and generative modeling perspectives are complementary
key_findings: Identifies three shifts: from local to global, from static snapshots to dynamics, and toward forward modeling
Argues current statistical neuroimaging cannot directly link to underlying neural mechanisms
Position paper review rather than empirical result set
interpretations: Dynamical systems perspective can enrich understanding of brain mechanisms in health and psychopathology
Embracing dynamics offers a mechanistic bridge between neuroimaging and behavior
